description: A person can live with disability or an ongoing condition and still have 'sunai', the condition of the functions and relations that sustain their life. Health takes its shape from the body and context being discussed. 'wolu' (healthy) describes that condition, while 'towe' (well) often reports how someone or a community is doing now.
articulatory_notes: Bring the tongue blade close to the alveolar ridge and pass unvoiced air through its central groove for 's', then raise the back of the tongue into high-back rounded 'u'. Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for dental 'n', carry the voice through the nose, and release into stressed open central 'a'. Raise the tongue into high-front unrounded 'i' for the final separate syllable.
usage_notes: Medical vocabulary can classify a disease, symptom, finding, or course without replacing the broader noun. Exact health measures remain in their source records.

semantic_domains.nature: the condition of life-sustaining functions in an organism or living system
semantic_domains.physical: the condition of a living body in its relevant context
